comment.text: This translation key displays the label "Open ID" in the application, likely used as a profile or identity type within OAuth settings. It may appear in UI elements such as profiles or room settings dialogs.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation 'هوية مفتوحة' means 'Open Identity' (as in an identity that is not closed/private), whereas 'OpenID' is a specific technical authentication protocol. In a technical context like OAuth settings, it should be transliterated or kept as the technical term.

The translation 'Açıq ID' means 'Open ID' in a literal sense (as in 'unlocked' or 'not closed'), but in the context of authentication (OpenID Connect/OAuth), 'OpenID' is a proper technical term/protocol name and should not be translated.
The translation 'Отворете ID' uses the imperative verb 'Open' (to open a door/file) instead of the technical term 'OpenID' (an authentication protocol/identity type).

The translation 'Otevřené ID' means 'Open ID' as in 'an ID that is open/unlocked'. In the context of OAuth/Authentication, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol name and should not be translated as an adjective-noun phrase.
The translation 'Άνοιγμα αναγνωριστικού' means 'Opening an identifier' (an action), whereas 'Open ID' in this context refers to the 'OpenID Connect' authentication protocol/standard.

The translation 'ID abierto' means 'Open ID' in the sense of an unlocked or accessible identity, whereas in the context of OAuth/Authentication, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol name that should not be translated literally.
The translation 'Ouvrir l'ID' means 'Open the ID' (as in an action to open a file or object), whereas 'Open ID' in this technical context refers to the 'OpenID' authentication protocol.
The Armenian translation 'Բացել ID-ն' means 'Open the ID' (as in an action/verb), whereas the English 'Open ID' refers to the authentication protocol (OpenID).
The translation 'Apri ID' uses the verb 'Apri' (to open), whereas 'OpenID' is a technical protocol/standard. In this context, it should be treated as a proper noun/technical term.

The Lao translation 'ເປີດ ID' means 'Open (verb) ID' (as in to open a door or a file), whereas the English 'Open ID' refers to the authentication protocol/identity standard. In a technical context like OAuth, 'OpenID' should be treated as a proper noun/technical term.
The translation 'Atvērt ID' means 'Open (verb) ID', whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol/standard. Translating 'Open' as a verb is a semantic error.

The translation 'ID aberto' means 'Open ID' in the sense of an ID that is not closed/locked. In the context of OAuth and authentication, 'OpenID' is a specific protocol name and should not be translated literally as an adjective-noun phrase.
The translation 'Abrir ID' uses the verb 'Abrir' (to open a file/door), whereas 'Open ID' in this technical context refers to the authentication protocol 'OpenID'.

The translation 'Deschidere ID' treats 'Open' as a verb (to open), whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol/standard. Translating it literally as 'opening an ID' is incorrect.
The translation 'ප්රවේශකය ID' translates to 'Access ID' or 'Entry ID', which is a different concept from 'Open ID' (an authentication protocol/standard).

The translation 'Otvorené ID' translates to 'Open ID' as in 'an identity that is open/unlocked'. In the context of OAuth/Authentication, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol name and should not be translated as an adjective-noun phrase.

The translation 'Odpri ID' treats 'Open' as a verb (to open), whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a proper noun/technical protocol name. Translating it as a verb changes the meaning from a technical identifier to a command.

The translation 'Отвори ID' means 'Open ID' as a command (verb + noun), whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a proper noun/protocol name. The current translation incorrectly treats 'Open' as a verb.

The translation 'Otvori ID' means 'Open ID' as a command (verb + noun), whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol name. 'Otvori' is a literal translation of the verb 'to open', which is incorrect for a technical term.

The translation 'Açık Kimlik' translates to 'Open Identity' literally, but in the context of OAuth and authentication (as indicated by the code context), 'OpenID' is a specific technical protocol name and should not be translated as a descriptive phrase.

The translation '打开 ID' means 'Open (verb) ID', whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a specific authentication protocol/standard. Translating 'Open' as a verb '打开' is a semantic error.
The translation 'Hapni ID' means 'Open the ID' (as a verb/command), whereas in the context of OAuth/Identity, 'OpenID' is a technical protocol/standard name that should not be translated as a verb.
